sections.paragraph:
ἕνεκα (Attic; PLond I, 42, 14 p. 30 [172 b.c.]; POxy 533, 25; 1293, 16f [other exx. in Mayser 242]; LXX [Thackeray 135]; OdeSol 11:17; Jos., Ant. 3, 107; this form Ac 26:21; Mt 19:5; Lk 6:22; Ac 19:32, but unanimously attested only in Ac 26:21. Also Mt 5:10, 19:29; Mk 13:9; Lk 21:12; Ro 8:36 [all only in some mss.]; 2 Cl 1:2; MPol 17:3; Hv 1, 3, 1; m 12, 4, 2), ἕνεκεν (increasingly prominent fr. III b.c. on: it is the prevailing form in ins [Meisterhans3-Schw. 217; Threatte II 666–68; Thieme 8; Hauser 21]; pap [Mayser 241]; LXX [Thackeray 82]; TestAbr A 17 p. 99, 10 [Stone p. 46]; EpArist; Ar. [Milne 74, 24]; Tat.; Ath., R.; and our lit.; Just. has this form, but only in citations), εἵνεκεν q.v. (Sb 1568, 7 [II b.c.]; PGiss 40 II, 21; PGM 5, 385; ins since 161 a.d. [Meisterhans3-Schw. 216]; LXX [Thackeray 82f]; Lk 4:18 [Is 61:1]; 18:29 v.l.; Ac 28:20 v.l.; 2 Cor 3:10; 7:12 v.l.; Hv 3, 2, 1), εἵνεκα (O. Wilck II 1148 [II b.c.]; En 101:2; later exx. in Crönert 114; Hv 3, 1, 9; Reinhold 39f). B-D-F §30, 3; 35, 3; W-S. §5, 20c; Mlt-H. 67f; 329f; Rob. index.

① functions as prep. w. gen. to indicate cause of or reason for someth., because of, on account of, for the sake of, Mt 5:10f; 10:18, 39; 16:25; 19:29; Mk 8:35; 10:29; 13:9; Lk 6:22; 9:24; 18:29; 21:12; Ac 28:20; Ro 8:36 (Ps 43:23); 14:20; 2 Cor 3:10; 7:12; 2 Cl 1:2; ITr 12:2; IPol 3:1; Pol 2:3; MPol 13:2; 17:3; Hv 1, 1, 6; 3, 1, 9; 3, 5, 2; m 5, 2, 2; 12, 4, 2; Hs 1:5; 9, 28, 5f; D 10:3; B 14:9; ἕ. τούτου for this reason (Lucian, D. Deor. 23, 2) Mt 19:5; Mk 10:7 (Gen 2:24); Hv 1, 3, 1; also ἕ. τούτων Ac 26:21 (PsSol 2:4). Used w. a rel. οὗ εἵ. (Musonius, Ep. 1, 11; Quint. Smyrn. 12, 227; PGiss 27 [117 a.d.]) Lk 4:18 (Is 61:1); τίνος ἕ.; why? (Demosth.; Menand., Epitr. 547 S. [371 Kö.]; Dio Chrys. 14 [31], 84; Tat. 9, 3) Ac 19:32; ἕ. τοῦ w. inf. foll. (Menand., Fgm. 358, 2 Kö.; Am 1:6; 2:4; 1 Esdr 8:21; Jos., Ant. 11, 293).

② marker of objective or purpose, in order that ἕ. τοῦ φανερωθῆναι τὴν σπουδὴν ὑμῶν in order that your zeal might be made known 2 Cor 7:12 (B-D-F §403; Rob. 1073).—DELG. M-M.

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

exx. exx. = examples

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

  1. fr. = from

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Threatte Threatte = LT., The Grammar of Attic Inscriptions I: Phonology; II: Morphology

Thieme Thieme = GT., Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander und das NT—List 3

Hauser Hauser = KH., Grammatik der griechischen Inschriften Lykiens—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. Wilck O. Wilck = UWilcken, ed., Griechische Ostraka aus Ägypten u. Nubien—List 4

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Reinhold Reinhold = HR., De graecitate patrum apostolicorum librorum apocryphorum novi testamenti etc.—List 6

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

prep. prep. = preposition(al)

gen. gen. = genitive

someth. someth. = something

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

Musonius Musonius = Gaius Musonius Rufus, I a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 334
